parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following point: • only the iodine concentration affects the rate OR so the concentrations of sulfuric acid and propanone do not affect the rate Allow so only the iodine concentration changes (significantly) Allow [H + ] and [CH COCH ] do not change (significantly) / (effectively) zero order (wrt [H + ] and [CH COCH ]) Ignore just concentrations of H SO and CH COCH are in excess Ignore comments on limiting reagents (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • to stop / quench the reaction Allow neutralise/remove the (sulfuric) acid/H + (catalyst) Ignore slow the reaction Do not award to remove OH - An answer that makes reference to the following point: • suitable nitrated methyl benzoate structure Accept displayed / structural / skeletal or any combination of Allow the 2-nitro or the 4-nitro isomer such as or Allow any di-nitrated isomer Allow any tri-nitrated isomer such as Ignore structure of methyl 3-nitrobenzoate Ignore water (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • no / needs filter paper (in the Buchner funnel) • no / needs side arm on the flask • no / needs pump (to reduce the pressure) (1) (1) (1) May be shown on diagram Ignore just no solid on filter paper Ignore just filter paper should be flat Allow needs a Buchner flask / no outlet on the flask Ignore just not connected (to pump) Do not award round bottom flask Allow vacuum / Venturi tube / tap vacuum Ignore flask should be extra thick to withstand vacuum (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • dissolve (solid) in the minimum (volume) of hot ethanol • (hot) (gravity) filtration to remove insoluble impurities • cool (solution) to precipitate the solid / methyl 3- nitrobenzoate • (suction) filtration to remove the soluble impurities (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow solvent for ethanol Ignore warm Allow crystallise Ignore any washing comments (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • difficult to separate the solid drying agent from the product Allow difficult to dry a solid with another solid Allow would contaminate or make product impure Ignore they are both solids Do not award reacts with the product (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • suitable drying method such as desiccator / (warm) oven Allow use filter paper / paper towel Allow leave (in a warm place) to dry Ignore leave in a cool place Do not award hot oven (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

Method 1 • evaluation of mass of methyl benzoate • evaluation of moles of methyl benzoate • evaluation of mass of methyl 3-nitrobenzoate and percentage yield or evaluation of moles of moles of methyl 3-nitrobenzoate and percentage yield Method 2 • evaluation of mass of methyl benzoate • evaluation of maximum mass • percentage yield (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation: m = (1.08 × 4 =) 4.32 (g) n = (4.32 ÷ 136 =) 0.031765 (mol) m = (0.0317647 × 181 =) 5.7494 / 5.75 (g) and % = ((3.05 ÷ 5.75) × 100 =) 53.043 / 53% n = (3.05 ÷ 181 =) 0.016851 (mol) and % = ((0.016851 ÷ 0.0317647) × 100 =) 53.043 / 53% Accept 53.049% from keeping values in calculator m = (1.08 × 4 =) 4.32 (g) m = ((181 ÷ 136) × 4.32 =) 5.7494 / 5.75 (g) % = ((3.05 ÷ 5.75) × 100 =) 53.043 / 53% Ignore SF except 1 SF Allow TE throughout but do not allow M3 if yield is higher than 100% Allow 53% scores 3 (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • any melting temperature range of 4 or more degrees and higher figure 70-79 o C and lower figure no lower than 65 o C • impurities make the melting temperature range lower and wider (1) (1) Mark independently Ignore comments about sharp (2) (Total for Question 4 = 16 marks) TOTAL FOR PAPER = 50 MARKS Pearson Education Limited.
